method: interactive verification run (design/flow simulation); web search cross-check (theverge.com direct fetch blocked; anthropic.com announcement page not fetched — Anthropic News has no RSS and the HTML fetcher is not built yet)

claims.text: Anthropic launched Claude Cowork on mobile and web in beta on 7 July 2026, rolling out to Max subscribers first
claims.reasoning: Reported independently and consistently by TechCrunch, VentureBeat and The Verge, each with own reporting beyond the press materials. Primary Anthropic page not directly captured (no RSS; HTML fetcher pending) — noted as a gap, acceptable for an announcement claim witnessed by 3+ Tier-2 outlets.

claims.text: Cowork tasks can run in the cloud and continue with no device online; work can be scheduled to run autonomously
claims.reasoning: Feature descriptions trace back to Anthropic's launch materials (independence collapse). No independent hands-on verification found at publication time.

claims.text: Anthropic's published usage data (1.2M anonymized sessions, 11–31 May, 600k+ organizations) puts software development at 8.7% of Cowork use; business-process work leads at 33.4%, content creation at 16.4%
claims.reasoning: Claim is about what Anthropic published (self-reported data, labeled as such in the article); figures quoted consistently by VentureBeat.

notes: Watch items: capture Anthropic's primary announcement URL once the HTML fetcher exists; flip cloud-continuation claim to confirmed on independent hands-on reports.
